description: Bulk up the knowledge base by adding new .mk knowledge files covering science, technology, geography, and history. Also fix the Makefile so that 'make' works correctly using single-compilation-unit approach (compile only mk_entry.cpp, not individual .o files that conflict). The vector search indexing is already wired into mk_entry.cpp and pattern_graph.cpp already loads the new .mk files -- we just need to create the actual knowledge files and fix the build system.
steps: Create MK_OS/ai_core/hre/knowledge_files/science_facts.mk with at least 40 high-quality facts covering physics, chemistry, biology, astronomy in the format: source|relation|target|weight (one fact per line, # for comments, grouped by section headers like '# === PHYSICS ===' etc.)
Create MK_OS/ai_core/hre/knowledge_files/technology_facts.mk with at least 40 facts about programming languages, operating systems, hardware, networking, AI concepts.
Create MK_OS/ai_core/hre/knowledge_files/geography_history.mk with at least 30 facts about major countries, capitals, historical events, famous people.

Fix MK_OS/Makefile: The main binary target should use single-compilation-unit approach: compile ONLY system/mk_entry.cpp (which #includes everything) into the mk_os binary. Remove the SOURCES/OBJECTS pattern that compiles individual .o files, since those conflict with the includes in mk_entry.cpp. Keep the tools targets as they are (those are standalone). Keep the clean, run, install, help targets. The main 'all' target should just do: $(CXX) $(CXXFLAGS) -o $(TARGET) $(ENTRY) $(LDFLAGS)
Verify the build works with both 'make' and the direct clang++ command.
acceptance_criteria: science_facts.mk exists with 40+ facts in correct .mk format
technology_facts.mk exists with 40+ facts in correct .mk format
geography_history.mk exists with 30+ facts in correct .mk format
The Makefile 'make' command successfully builds the mk_os binary without linker errors
The direct build command also works: clang++ -std=c++17 -O2 -DDARWIN -o mk_os system/mk_entry.cpp -lcurl -lsqlite3
findings: Build succeeds with 11 warnings (all pre-existing: unused parameters, field reorder, unused private fields). No errors. The Makefile fix was straightforward - replaced SOURCES/OBJECTS pattern with direct compilation of ENTRY file. All three knowledge files created with well over the minimum required facts (173, 210, 175 respectively). The prior feature changes (pattern_graph.cpp loadFromFile calls and mk_entry.cpp vector indexing) were uncommitted and included in this commit.
